tasks_completed.changes: Added _SCRIPT_INTERPS frozenset with 17 interpreter base names (including both 'Rscript'/'R' and 'rscript'/'r' after codex review found case-sensitivity bug)
tasks_completed.rationale: Named set needed for O(1) membership test in _is_script_interp()

tasks_completed.description: Added _is_script_interp() helper for versioned interpreter name detection
tasks_completed.changes: Added _is_script_interp(name) using re.split(r'[\d.]', name)[0] to strip version suffix before lookup
tasks_completed.rationale: Enables python3.12, node20, etc. to be recognized without enumerating all version strings

tasks_completed.changes: _SHELL_METAS = frozenset('<>()\`'); _process_script_interp strips word-kind tokens after cmd_idx that contain no shell metacharacters, preserving tokens with metas (process substitution), stripping single/double quoted strings as usual
tasks_completed.rationale: Eliminates false positives from argv data tokens like 'rm' and 'killall' following a non-shell interpreter

tasks_completed.changes: Added _is_script_interp(cmd_word) branch before DANGER_COMMANDS check in _process_segment()
tasks_completed.rationale: When interpreter is non-shell, all following argv tokens are data, not commands

tasks_completed.description: Added TestScriptInterpArgStripping test class with 9 test cases
tasks_completed.changes: 7 spec-mandated tests + 2 additional tests for R/Rscript case sensitivity (codex finding #4)
tasks_completed.rationale: Proves the fix works and prevents regression; R/Rscript tests address real bug found by codex

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — new feature, not a regression fix
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The _process_segment() else-branch kept all word-kind tokens visible, including interpreter subcommands like 'rm' and 'killall' that are pure argv data
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: New _process_script_interp() branch strips word-kind tokens after the interpreter cmd_idx when they contain no shell metacharacters; tokens with metas (<>()`) are preserved for process substitution detectability

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 45 lines in bash_context_strip.py are all functional: frozenset (8L), helper (4L), dispatch (3L), new function (22L), constant (1L), plus docstring. 40 lines in tests are 9 test methods mandated by spec plus 2 additional tests for codex-found R/Rscript bug. No padding, no refactoring.

dev.qa_notes: Run: cd hooks && python3 -m pytest tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v. All 49 tests must pass (40 pre-existing + 9 new). Verify behavioral smoke: CMD_INPUT='python3 manage.py rm session-123' python3 hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py should produce output without 'rm' as a standalone word. Also verify CMD_INPUT='bash -c rm /tmp/foo' still includes rm.
dev.implementation_summary: Added _SCRIPT_INTERPS branch in _process_segment() to eliminate false positive danger-token matches. When cmd_word is a non-shell script interpreter (python3, node, ruby, etc.), word-kind tokens after cmd_idx that contain no shell metacharacters are stripped as pure argv data. Tokens with metacharacters (<>()`) are preserved for process substitution detectability. Codex adversarial review found R/Rscript case-sensitivity bug (lowercase r/rscript in frozenset but canonical commands are uppercase R/Rscript); fixed and 2 additional tests added.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 7 findings. Finding #4 (R/Rscript case sensitivity) was in-scope real bug: _SCRIPT_INTERPS only had lowercase 'r'/'rscript' but canonical R commands are 'R' and 'Rscript'. Findings #1,#2,#3,#5,#6,#7 are pre-existing limitations in _split_top_level() and _find_command_word() outside this fix's scope. Finding #7 (spaced process substitution) is a gap in _process_script_interp() but requires paren-depth tracking across multiple tokens and was not in the BA spec. OBSERVATION_ONLY: _is_script_interp() overbroad for names like 'r2' — accepted as minor safety-false-negative risk outside scope.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Fixed finding #4: added 'R' and 'Rscript' to _SCRIPT_INTERPS frozenset. Added 2 tests (test_rscript_canonical_case_stripped, test_r_canonical_case_stripped) covering this fix. Findings #1,2,3,5,6,7 logged as out_of_scope adjacent issues for separate cycle.

codex_observation_only: _is_script_interp() splits on [\d.] which matches 'r2' or 'python3rm' as interpreters — minor false-negative risk outside the false-positive threat model this fix addresses
adjacent_issues_for_separate_cycle: Finding #1: _split_top_level() does not honor backslash-escaped separators (pre-existing bug in unchanged function)
Finding #2: _find_command_word() does not handle quoted/expanded env-assignment prefixes before interpreter (pre-existing limitation)
Finding #3: Quoted interpreter command words like '"python3"' are not recognized (pre-existing limitation)
Finding #5: Wrapper options (env -i, timeout N) hide effective interpreter from _find_command_word (pre-existing limitation)
Finding #6: Single & background operator not handled as separator in _split_top_level (pre-existing bug)
Finding #7: Spaced process substitution <( rm /tmp/x ) — rm inside is stripped because tokens between <( and ) have no metacharacters (gap in _process_script_interp; requires paren-depth tracking)

self_verification.notes: 49/49 tests pass. Verified CMD_INPUT='python3 manage.py rm session-123' python3 hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py produces 'python3   ' (rm stripped). CMD_INPUT='bash -c rm /tmp/foo' produces 'bash -c rm /tmp/foo' (rm preserved in shell interp).

recommendations: Address codex finding #7 (spaced process substitution) in a separate cycle by adding paren-depth tracking to _process_script_interp()
Address codex finding #1 (escaped separators) in _split_top_level() in a separate cycle
Consider adding wrapper option parsing for env/timeout/nice to _find_command_word() (codex finding #5)
